name: external-system-router
title: External System Router
version_notes: Closes exact-target, graphical-approval, secret-rejection, capability-ledger, n8n admission, and drift-review gaps with focused regressions.

outputs.notes: Complete reviewed control-plane references and schemas.
Complete deterministic router, n8n admission, and drift scripts.

skill_creation_review.existing_skill_review: Reviewed external-system-router against ai-coding-agent-rules, toolkit-setup, n8n-agent-rules, n8n-local-setup, n8n-workflow-helper-scripts, project-completion-audit, self-hosted-service-safety, codex-ssh-hostinger-coolify-setup-maintainer, secure-cicd-installer, managed-app-foundation-review, source-watch, native plugin metadata, and the local bridge. Existing skills own domain safety or setup but none owns one reusable operation-level provider target, envelope, capability-audit, graphical approval, reconciliation, receipt, and route lifecycle contract.
skill_creation_review.trigger: Provider APIs/CLIs/SDKs, MCP/connectors, provider-console browser/computer-use, deployment, DNS, database, storage, workflow, OAuth, provider setup/capability selection, operation receipts, or supported continuation.
skill_creation_review.decision_reason: The cross-provider operation router has a distinct universal trigger and safety boundary. Folding it into setup, Coolify, n8n, CI/CD, or self-hosted skills would duplicate policy and leave other provider operations uncovered.
skill_creation_review.unique_value: Broad tool permission with narrow task authority; per-operation strongest-admissible routing; universal graphical disclosure; last-resort history; repo-and-intent reconciliation; host-neutral target profiles; redacted receipts; staged route migration.
skill_creation_review.runtime_footprint: One compact safety-router SKILL, focused references, dependency-free Node runtime, and versioned JSON schemas. Provider-specific adapters remain separate projects/PRs.
skill_creation_review.local_assets: No binaries, external packages, vendor code, credentials, provider output, screenshots, or live state.
skill_creation_review.output_contract: Produce a validated envelope, target binding, per-operation capability audit and route, complete graphical disclosure when needed, verified result, redacted receipt, and completion ledger.
skill_creation_review.anti_bloat_review: Not a generic executor and does not absorb provider-specific endpoint code. Detailed contracts remain in routed references and schemas instead of root instructions.
skill_creation_review.safety_boundary: Live/external mutation, graphical control, credentials, private data, deployment, DNS, database, workflow, and destructive operations remain risk-tiered and owner-authorised. Scheduled paths are read-only.
skill_creation_review.third_party_audit: not_applicable_first_party; official documentation supplied facts only and no third-party skill or executable material was imported.
skill_creation_review.publisher_workflow: Used context-preserving-ai-publisher for a source-first first-party project, deterministic exact publication from _main, routing/safety-matrix updates, plugin version alignment, sync, targeted tests, and final security review.
skill_creation_review.routing: external-system-router is routed as the implicit universal safety gate for external-system and graphical-control operations; provider-specific implementation remains in companion skills/adapters.

project.name: External System Router
project.summary: Risk-scoped external-system routing, progressive capability reconciliation, graphical-control approval, host-neutral target profiles, and redacted operation receipts.

skill.summary: Routes exact owner-authorised external-system operations through risk tiers and the strongest admissible operation-specific interface.
